name: Open Dental Practice Management API
what_it_does: Open Dental REST API for open-source dental practice management software. Enables AI agents to manage patient records and demographics, retrieve appointment schedules, access treatment planning data, retrieve financial and insurance claim data, manage provider schedules, integrate with dental imaging systems, and connect practice data with external healthcare and billing systems.
use_cases: Retrieving patient appointment schedules from dental practice workflow agents
Managing patient demographic records from front desk automation agents
Accessing treatment plan and procedure data from clinical coordination agents
Retrieving insurance claim status from dental billing agents
not_for: Non-dental medical practices without dental-specific workflow needs
Large health system dental networks requiring enterprise EMR integration
Orthodontic-only practices without general dental management workflows
best_when: A dental practice or DSO (dental service organization) using Open Dental wants AI agents to automate scheduling, patient communication, treatment coordination, and billing workflows.
avoid_when: HIPAA: Patient dental health records are Protected Health Information (PHI). All integrations must comply with HIPAA Security Rule requirements. Open Dental is on-premises — network security is practice responsibility.

auth.notes: Open Dental uses API key authentication. Keys are practice-scoped. Local network API by default — must configure API server settings. Self-hosted on-premises software requires practice to manage API server configuration.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source — software free; API included; support and cloud hosting optional paid services
pricing.paid_tiers: Monthly support fees; Open Dental Cloud (hosted) paid separately
pricing.notes: Jordan, Utah company. Open-source dental software — most affordable practice management option. 40,000+ practices using Open Dental. Strong community and developer ecosystem. On-premises or cloud hosting available.

performance.rate_limits: No documented rate limits — on-premises local API

known_agent_gotchas: HIPAA: Patient dental records are PHI — all integrations require HIPAA compliance framework
No public MCP server — local REST API hosted by the practice itself
On-premises software — network architecture and API exposure is practice responsibility
No granular scopes — API key grants full practice data access
No webhooks — requires polling for appointment and schedule changes
TLS enforcement depends on practice network configuration — verify SSL setup
agent_readiness.security_notes: HIPAA: Patient dental PHI. On-premises deployment — practice responsible for API server security. API key only, no scopes. TLS configuration practice-dependent.

security_score_components.security_notes: Patient dental PHI. HIPAA required. On-premises API — practice manages security posture. API key only, no scopes.
